stages.name: unit_tests
stages.evidence: test/ai_command_test.zig 'explain context redacts absolute paths and secret tokens in every field (F-4)' builds the prompt context (buildPromptValue) from a report whose file is an absolute developer path embedding a GitHub token and whose original/diff carry the token, and asserts neither survives and redactions_applied is non-empty with absolute_path + secret_value labels; 'explain rendered output no longer echoes...' asserts the rendered text/json body is clean; 'doctest survivor context redacts...' covers buildSurvivorContextValue. Both context tests failed before the wiring (file/secret present, redactions_applied []). test/ai_context_test.zig adds normalizeAbsolutePaths (absolute->placeholder, relative + division preserved) and redactField (path + secret + label logging) unit tests. Full suite green (518 tests).

stages.name: real_binary_behavior
stages.command: zig build && ./zig-out/bin/zentinel explain m_leak --ai-provider stub --input-report report.json  (report.file = absolute path embedding a secret)
stages.evidence: Before the fix the rendered stdout echoed the absolute path '/Users/dev/work-ghp_.../calc.zig' (secret segment included) verbatim in the summary and next-action lines (2 occurrences each). After the fix the same command prints 'Mutant m_leak in <path> ...' with the path normalized to <path> and zero occurrences of /Users/ or the ghp_ token.

stages.name: mutation_checks
stages.reason: Task 120 widens deterministic-core AI-context redaction scope; the fix is proven by failing-before/passing-after context + rendered-output unit tests plus a real-binary before/after stdout proof. Full self-mutation dogfood remains gated to task 085.

residual_risk: Redaction now covers every path/source/diff-bearing field (mutant.file/original/replacement/diff; doctest file/source_ref/mutated_diff; diagnostic file/message; doc paths; evidence excerpts) and the stub responses that echo a path, in both the mutation (command.zig) and doctest (doctest_command.zig) flows. The shared primitives live in redaction.zig (normalizeAbsolutePaths, built-in match reporting) and context.zig (RedactionLog, redactField, redactStrArray, redactAndCapLogged), all deterministic_core; redaction is derived only from input data, so AI output cannot influence any deterministic field (I-001) -- the determinism boundary is unchanged.

Absolute-path normalization is intentionally conservative: it replaces an absolute multi-segment path token with <path> and leaves relative paths and a lone division operator intact, so the AI still sees the mutated code (the non-goal forbids redacting code to uselessness). A single-segment absolute path (e.g. `/usr` alone, rare in source) is not normalized; secret-value scrubbing still applies everywhere.

privacy.redactions_applied is now truthful: it records configured patterns that matched plus the synthetic labels absolute_path and secret_value, and is non-empty exactly when a redaction occurred. Redaction fails closed (error.RedactionFailed -> AiResponseInvalid) when a configured pattern is malformed.
stubSurvivorExplain echoes only refs/ids/operator (no path), so it needed no change; the suggest response's projectRelative validation still applies on top of the redaction.
